## Shortest-path verification (from `mcp__graphify__shortest_path`)
- `run()` â†’ `run_inline()` â†’ `mark_indexed()` â†’ `builder.rs`  (3 hops)
- `run_leiden_pass()` â†’ `run_inline()` â†’ `mark_indexed()` â†’ `builder.rs`  (3 hops)
- `run_deps_pass()`   â†’ `run_inline()` â†’ `mark_indexed()` â†’ `builder.rs`  (3 hops)

## Caveats from the MCP graph
- The graph could not resolve a path to a node literally named `sqlite`/`sqlx`. The SQLite-touching layer in this repo is the `store` crate (`store\src\builder.rs` + `store\src\schema.rs`), reached via `mark_indexed()` and the `*_pass` functions.
- Edges marked `[I]` (inferred) include `run_inline â†’ mark_indexed` and the `init_shard/init_meta â†’ schema_sql/apply_migrations` links â€” graphify is confident enough to surface them but they are not direct extracted call edges, so individual pass functions likely make additional direct calls into `store::` that the graph did not extract.
- Several `*_pass` functions (multimodal, embedding, leiden, deps, audit, etc.) almost certainly issue their own SQL through the `store` crate, but those edges were not present in the extracted graph â€” only the terminal `mark_indexed()` write-back is captured.
